summary: Large decompilation/matching PR for sysdolphin baselib `sislib`, adding about 2000 lines of implementation and expanding `sislib.h` with provisional allocator/text-rendering structs and typed prototypes. It also updated two `mn` menu users, `mndeflicker.c` and `mnhyaku.c`, to consume the new `sislib_UnkAllocReturn` type instead of local `struct sislib_idk` float-array stubs. The author noted that struct and variable names would improve while working through `mn` files. Evidence includes one issue comment suggesting a failed build could likely be fixed with `tools/revert_asm.py`; no formal review comments were present in the slice.
what_changed: Created a substantial `src/sysdolphin/baselib/sislib.c` implementation from an effectively empty file, adding includes, globals, allocator/free-list routines, text/SIS object lifecycle routines, camera/GObj-related setup, archive cleanup, and rendering-related functions shown in the diff excerpt.

Expanded `sislib.h` with concrete provisional structs: `sislib_UnkAllocData`, `sislib_UnkAllocReturn`, and `sislib_UnkAlloc3`, including fields such as `Vec4 x14`, `Vec2 x24`, `U8Vec4 x30`, `HSD_GObj* x54`, `SIS* x5C`, and linked-list pointers.
Replaced local `struct sislib_idk { float idk[40]; }` definitions in `mndeflicker.c` and `mnhyaku.c` with shared `sislib_UnkAllocReturn*` usage from `sislib.h`.
Updated menu code to access named vector fields `temp_r3_6->x24.x` and `temp_r3_6->x24.y` instead of raw float-array indices `idk[9]` and `idk[10]`.

Added and corrected multiple `HSD_SisLib_*` prototypes in `sislib.h`, including typed arguments/returns for `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC`, `HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4`, `HSD_SisLib_803A6368`, `HSD_SisLib_803A7F0C`, `HSD_SisLib_803A8134`, and `HSD_SisLib_803A84BC`.
Changed `HSD_SisLib_8040C4B8` from `UNK_T` to `HSD_CObjDesc` and added `baselib/cobj.h` plus Dolphin matrix type includes to support the new typed declarations.

Commented out the `gobj.h` declaration `extern HSD_GObj* HSD_SisLib_804D797C` with a todo asking whether it is actually correct, while `sislib.h` introduces `HSD_SisLib_804D797C` as `sislib_UnkAlloc3*`.
Added a `PAD_STACK(4)` in `mnDeflicker_8024A4BC`, indicating a stack-layout/matching adjustment.
Added a comment in `mnhyaku.c` noting that `GET_GOBJ_USER_DATA` appears to be a common inline in `mn`.
smart_moves: Promoted a repeated local unknown struct from menu files into a shared `sislib.h` type, reducing duplicated speculative definitions and making future users converge on one layout.

Used partial semantic structure fields (`Vec2`, `Vec4`, `U8Vec4`, `HSD_GObj*`, `SIS*`) while preserving unknown-offset names like `x24` and `x54`, which improves type safety without overclaiming final names.
Updated call sites to rely on the typed return of `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC` instead of explicit casts from `UNK_T`/`void*`, making downstream code cleaner and more informative.

Kept uncertainty visible through `@todo` comments for names, user-data interpretation, and the questionable `HSD_SisLib_804D797C` declaration rather than pretending the structures were fully understood.
Identified a repeated `GET_GOBJ_USER_DATA` macro/inline pattern in `mn`, which is useful for later cleanup or common-header extraction.
Used `PAD_STACK(4)` as a targeted matching tactic rather than contorting local variables solely to satisfy stack layout.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling a central baselib module, immediately update dependent callers to use the new shared structs; this validates layouts against real call sites and prevents drift from local placeholder types.
Provisional struct names such as `sislib_UnkAllocReturn` are acceptable when paired with offset-preserving field names and comments documenting uncertainty.
Replacing raw array-index access with vector fields can clarify intent and still preserve matching when the offset mapping is known, as with `idk[9]`/`idk[10]` becoming `x24.x`/`x24.y`.
Header fixes are part of matching work: typed prototypes, forward declarations, and correct includes can remove casts and make unrelated files easier to match.
Conflicting global declarations across headers should be resolved cautiously; if the correct type is uncertain, leaving an explicit todo/comment is better than exporting a likely wrong declaration.
Build failures during matching may sometimes be handled by reverting generated assembly with `tools/revert_asm.py`, according to the single issue comment in this PR.
naming_conventions: Used `sislib_` prefix for newly introduced provisional types, e.g. `sislib_UnkAllocData`, `sislib_UnkAllocReturn`, and `sislib_UnkAlloc3`.
Kept DOL-address-style names for unmatched functions and globals, e.g. `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC`, `HSD_SisLib_804D7978`, and `HSD_SisLib_8040C4B8`.
Used offset-like field names (`x0`, `x24`, `x54`, `x9F`) for unknown struct fields, with selective comments where behavior was suspected.
Used `fn_` names for functions that were not yet confidently assigned an `HSD_SisLib_` symbol in the header, e.g. `fn_803A60EC` and `fn_803A6FEC`.
Left explicit `@todo` comments beside uncertain names and types instead of converting them to speculative final names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added `PAD_STACK(4)` in `mnDeflicker_8024A4BC` to influence stack layout for matching.
Typed the return of `HSD_SisLib_803A5ACC` as `sislib_UnkAllocReturn*`, allowing menu call sites to remove casts and match through structured field access.
Converted placeholder float-array indexing to structured `Vec2` member access while preserving the same offsets.
Added typed function prototypes in `sislib.h` so callers compile with consistent ABI expectations.
Issue comment suggested using `tools/revert_asm.py` as a possible fix for a failed build, but the slice does not show the exact failure or resolution.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suspected the failed build could be fixed by using `tools/revert_asm.py`.
review_feedback.evidence: PR issue comment excerpt: "I haven't looked at it closely yet but I suspect the failed build can be fixed by using `tools/revert_asm.py`."

review_feedback.author: thefoxcam
review_feedback.feedback: Struct and variable names were expected to improve while continuing work in `mn` files.
review_feedback.evidence: PR body excerpt: "Will improve names for structs and variables as I work on files in `mn`."

follow_up_queries: Check later PRs touching `sislib_UnkAllocReturn` for improved field names and confirmed semantics.
Search for later resolution of the `HSD_SisLib_804D797C` type conflict between `gobj.h` and `sislib.h`.
Search `mn` files for repeated `GET_GOBJ_USER_DATA` inline/macro patterns noted by this PR.
Inspect later sislib PRs for replacement of `sislib_UnkAllocData`, `sislib_UnkAlloc3`, and `fn_803A60EC` provisional names.
Look for commits around PR 1421 that mention `tools/revert_asm.py` to confirm whether it fixed the failed build.
